perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "AFFILIATE" means, in the case of any corporation, partnership, limited liability company or other business or investment entity, a different business entity or other individual that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with the business entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "AFFILIATE" means any entity that has a business relationship with either party, regardless of the nature of that relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by broadening the definition of 'Affiliate' from entities under direct or indirect control to any entity with a business relationship. This contradicts sections relying on a specific control-based definition, introducing ambiguity and legal uncertainty when determining affiliate rights and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) NON SOLICITATION. During the Term of this Agreement and during the three year period after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Licensee will not solicit any person employed by Licensor and/or its Affiliates to leave his or her employment with Licensor. For purpose of this Article the term "Solicit" means any affirmative recruitment specifically aimed at one or more individuals identified by name, title or affiliation, but shall not mean generally advertising job openings or any activities that constitute follow-up to individuals who respond to job opening advertisements or who voluntarily initiate employment inquiries.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of "AFFILIATE" was based on control, whereas the changed definition broadens it to any entity with a business relationship. This directly contradicts the non-solicitation clause, which restricts solicitation of employees of the Licensor and its Affiliates. Under the original definition, the scope of 'Affiliates' was limited to entities under common control. Now, under the changed definition, it includes potentially any entity with any business relationship, drastically broadening the non-solicitation restriction and creating a direct conflict in how the non-solicitation clause is applied. This is a clear contradiction as it fundamentally alters the scope of the restricted parties.

perturbation.original_text: III. Time of Payment (a) Each payment for a particular calendar month shall be due no later than the (20th) day of the subsequent calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: III. Time of Payment (a) Each payment for a particular calendar month shall be due no later than the (15th) day of the subsequent calendar month.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the payment deadline from the 20th to the 15th of the subsequent month, creating an in-text contradiction with any other sections that might refer to the original payment terms. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes over late payment penalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV. Record Keeping and Reports (a) As a condition of Licensee receiving the grant of a license herein, Licensee agrees to maintain reasonable records relating to all trades conducted through the Licensed Technology and all other uses of the Licensed Technology by the Licensee under this Agreement. Licensee shall prepare and submit summary quarterly reports to Licensor no later than 20 days following the last business day of each calendar quarter, which reports must specify a complete record of usage of the Licensed Technology by the Licensee and shall accompany the payments made to Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified payments were due no later than the 20th of the subsequent month. The changed text alters this to the 15th. The contradicted text states that quarterly reports must accompany the payments and be submitted no later than 20 days following the last business day of each calendar quarter. Changing the payment due date to the 15th directly contradicts the report submission deadline tied to the payment, creating a clear inconsistency and potential dispute over late fees and reporting obligations. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: I Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period").
perturbation.changed_text: I Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 30 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period").
perturbation.explanation: The modified text reduces the initial license period from 36 months to 30 months. This directly contradicts any references to the original 36-month term elsewhere in the agreement, particularly those related to payment schedules, renewal options, or termination clauses based on the initial period. This inserted perturbation creates uncertainty regarding the contract's duration and affects several clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Rebate Shares. During the period commencing on the Acceptance Date through the thirty six (36th) month anniversary of this Agreement, the Licensor shall remit to Licensee a series of rebates based upon the amount of Notional Volumes traded on the EPath FX Trading Platform by Licensee. The computation of the rebates received by Licensee shall be expressed as a fixed percentage (15%) of Billable Platform Fees paid by Licensee to Licensor. The rebates shall be payable in shares of Licensor's Common Stock; priced at $.25 cents per share. The amount of rebates earned by Licensee shall be calculated for each tranche of 30 Billion of Notional Volume traded in the EPath FX Platform by Licensee. The amount of shares earned by Licensee shall terminate at the earlier of, the expiration of the term of this Agreement or the receipt of Six (6,000,000) Million shares of Licensor's common stock by Licensee. By way of illustration, if the Licensee trades 30 Billion of Notional Volume on the Platform, with a Billable Platform Fee of $279,000. The rebate to Licensee will be calculated as follows: Rebate = 15% of $279,000 = $41,850; Shares of Licensor representing $41,850/$.25 = 167,400 shares of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the initial license period from 36 months to 30 months. The contradicted text specifies rebate shares being remitted "through the thirty six (36th) month anniversary of this Agreement". By reducing the term to 30 months, the Licensee would no longer be eligible for rebates through the 36th month, creating a direct conflict regarding the agreement's duration and rebate eligibility.
